turns.user_message: Someone casually mentioned how I keep checking the locks, and it completely threw me off balance. I never really noticed it before, but now I can't stop thinking about it every single time I walk through the door. It just started a few days ago, and suddenly my hands feel shaky whenever I try to leave the house. I keep second-guessing every single little movement I make, wondering if I missed something completely obvious. It feels like my tired brain is stuck on a loop, replaying that simple comment over and over until I get completely tangled up in it.

That offhand remark about my habits has completely taken over my thoughts, and I find myself pacing around the room for hours. Every little noise outside makes my chest tighten because I keep imagining what could go wrong if I am not perfectly prepared. I try to distract myself with simple routines, but my mind just keeps circling back to that moment. It feels like a heavy weight is sitting right on my ribs, making it hard to catch my breath. I keep checking things repeatedly just to quiet the noise in my head, but the relief never actually lasts more than a few seconds.

I still hear those exact words echoing in my ears, and now my whole body feels completely wired and exhausted at the same time. Every single step I take feels like walking on thin ice, and I constantly brace myself for something terrible to happen out of nowhere. My heart pounds so hard it actually hurts, and I can't seem to slow down my breathing no matter how hard I try. I keep running through every possible bad scenario until my thoughts blur together into this overwhelming static. It feels like I am completely trapped inside my own head, and I just want the constant buzzing to finally stop.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This just started recently.
